coredata.dc:title: Magnetic solid phase extraction of erythrosine (E127) in pharmaceutical samples with Fe3O4/C-nanodots hybrid material prior to spectrophotometric analysis
coredata.dc:creator: Emiroğlu, Elifcan
Yuvali, Dönay
Sarp, Gökhan
Yilmaz, Erkan
Narin, İbrahim
coredata.dc:description: In this study, a green production method was used to obtain magnetic carbon nanodot hybrid material (Fe3O4@C-nanodots). The new material was used as adsorbent for the magnetic solid phase extraction (MSPE) of Erythrosine (E127) in pharmaceutical samples prior to UV–VIS spectrophotometry analysis. C-nanodots were produced from pasteurized cow milk by using a simple and cheap hydrothermal synthesis method. After production of the C-nanodots, Fe3O4@C-nanodot hybrid material was fabricated with one-step hydrothermal method in green solvent medium. Experimental variables affecting the extraction efficiency of erythrosine such as pH of sample solution, amount of adsorbent, extraction time, eluent type and volume were studied and optimized in details. The characterization studies for the Fe3O4@C-nanodots were carried out by X-ray diffraction spectrometry (XRD), Raman spectrometry (Raman) and Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) analysis. Under the optimum experimental conditions, The limit of detection (LOD) was 60 ng·L−1 and the recoveries at three spiked levels were ranged from 94.8% to 97.6% with the relative standard deviation (RSD) less than 5.0%. The results show that the combination MSPE with UV–VIS spectrophotometer provided a simple and rapid method for extraction and determination of Erythrosine.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Carbon nanodots
Erythrosine
Magnetic solid phase extraction
Preconcentration
Separation
UV–Vis spectrophotometer

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis of magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots hybrid material
Magnetic solid phase extraction (MSPE) procedure for erythrosine
Effect of the amount of adsorbent
Effect of elution solvent type and volume
Effect of vortex time on adsorption process
Effect of vortex time on desorption process
Analytical performance of the proposed MSPE method
Application of MSPE method to pharmaceutical samples

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: FE-SEM images of C-nanodot nanoparticles (A) and FE-SEM images of Fe3O4@C-nanodot hybrid material [28].
XRD spectra of C-nanodots (A) and magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots (B).
Effect of pH on the magnetic solid phase extraction efficiency of erytrosine (N = 3).
Effect of the amount of Fe3O4@C-nanodot on the magnetic solid phase extraction efficiency of Erythrosine (N = 3).
Isotherm parameters for adsorption of erythrosine onto Fe3O4-C nanodots.
Analytical performance values of the developed MSPE-UV–Vis procedure.
Addition-Recovery experiments results of erythrosine for pharmaceutical samples (N = 3).
Comparison of the analytical performance of the method developed for erythrosine with other extraction methods in the literature.

row.entry: Cr (VI) Reagent in Micellar Media Using Mean Centering of Ratio Spectra
Switchable-hydrophilicity solvent liquid–liquid microextraction prior to magnetic nanoparticle-based dispersive solid-phase microextraction

head.ce:title: Magnetic solid phase extraction of erythrosine (E127) in pharmaceutical samples with Fe3O4/C-nanodots hybrid material prior to spectrophotometric analysis

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In this study, a green production method was used to obtain magnetic carbon nanodot hybrid material (Fe3O4@C-nanodots). The new material was used as adsorbent for the magnetic solid phase extraction (MSPE) of Erythrosine (E127) in pharmaceutical samples prior to UV–VIS spectrophotometry analysis. C-nanodots were produced from pasteurized cow milk by using a simple and cheap hydrothermal synthesis method. After production of the C-nanodots, Fe3O4@C-nanodot hybrid material was fabricated with one-step hydrothermal method in green solvent medium. Experimental variables affecting the extraction efficiency of erythrosine such as pH of sample solution, amount of adsorbent, extraction time, eluent type and volume were studied and optimized in details. The characterization studies for the Fe3O4@C-nanodots were carried out by X-ray diffraction spectrometry (XRD), Raman spectrometry (Raman) and Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) analysis. Under the optimum experimental conditions, The limit of detection (LOD) was 60 ng·L−1 and the recoveries at three spiked levels were ranged from 94.8% to 97.6% with the relative standard deviation (RSD) less than 5.0%. The results show that the combination MSPE with UV–VIS spectrophotometer provided a simple and rapid method for extraction and determination of Erythrosine.

ce:section.ce:para: Colorants are defined as substances that regulate the color of foods and drugs or participate in color. Coloring is done to make the product more attractive and consumable [1]. The colorants used in various products are obtained from chemical and natural sources [2]. Erythrosine (E127) is know Red No.3 is a xanthene dye that widely used as a color additive in foods, cosmetics and pharmaceuticals products [3,4]. Erythrosine is a dyestuff lends a pink shade to applications. Erythrosine is provided chemically disodium 2-(2,4,5,7-tetraiodo-6-oxido-3-oxo-3H-xanthen-9-yl) benzoate by manifacturer. The molar mass is 879.86 g·moL-1 and melting point is 142–144 °C. The solubility of Erythrosine is 100 g·L-1 in water. The pKa of Eryhtrosine is 4.1 at 25 °C [3].

Erythrosine is permitted as a food and pharmaceutical dyestuff in China, the European Union, the USA and other regions. The amount acceptable daily intake (ADI) of Erythrosine is collocated by Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (JECFA) /World Health Organization (WHO) and Eurepan UnitedCommission Regulation. JECFA and EU are confirmed acceptable daily intake of 0–0.1 mg·kg−1 of body weight [5,6]. The utilization of erythrosine is permitted, however it is toxic to pituitary and spermatogenesis processes, the genotoxicity and mutagenicity of erythrosine [5–8]. Because of these adverse effects of erythrosine on health, it is important to develop methods for accurate and sensitive analysis in pharmaceutical and food samples.

Many sensitive and rapid analysis methods such as liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (LC-MS) [9], thin layer chromatography (TLC) [10], high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) [11–13], capillary electrophoresis (CE) [14,15], spectrophotometry [16–18] have been developed for the determination of colorants in beverage, food, pharmaceutical and environmental samples. The instrumental analysis methods could not applied to samples directly. Therefore, sample preparation is one of the most important stage for determination of analytes [19,20].

The conventional sample extraction techniques such as solid-phase extraction (SPE) and liquid–liquid extraction (LLE) have some important disadvantages that LLE and SPE are require high volume of toxic solvents or sorbents, need to expensive and specific sorbent for the extraction of analytes, involving time-consuming and multi-step experimental processes and build up large amount of waste [20,21]. The disadvantages cause to develop new extraction methods and adsorbents. Magnetic solid phase extraction methods, which are classified as new generation sample preparation methods, have gained an important place in the literature as they can be applied to many materials as well as eliminating these disadvantages.

Nanomaterials (NMs) have attained attention in technological development due to their excellent physical, chemical and biological properties with their performance over scientific advances. NMs are categorized their size, composition, shape, and origin. Due to increased of production of NMs and their industrial and biological applications, new synthesis methods of NMs are developed by researchers [22]. These methods are that Fe3O4 magnetic nanoparticles are used as adsorbents, have important usage advantages such as completing the extraction process in a shorter time by using less amount of adsorbent with large surface area, using less solvent, and most importantly, ease of magnetic separation. However, due to the high hydrophilic character of Fe3O4 nanoparticles, their agglomeration in solution environment limits their use for a long time. Carbon-based materials (C-nanodots, carbon nanotubes, graphene, graphene oxide and the like), polymers, deep eutectic solvents, ionic liquids and metal organic frameworks are good options for obtaining magnetic nanoparticles that can be used for a long time [23]. Among these materials, C-nanodots, which are a new member of nanomaterials, have started to be used for many applications such as sample preparation, drug and gene release/transport, imaging, sensor, supercapacitor and so on [24]. The most important reason why C-nanodots are widely used in the literature is that they can be produced from different vegetable, fruit and animal sources containing carbon by simple synthesis methods [25]. C-nanodots as new adsorbents have some properties that reusability, maximum adsorption capacity due to high surface area, high selectivity and minimum price when compared with classic adsorbents [26].

In this work, a green and simple route was used to fabricate magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots hybrid material as MSPE adsorbent for the separation and preconcentration of erythrosine from pharmaceutical tablet and syrup samples. Erythrosine concentration in last phase was determined with UV–Vis spectrophotometer.

Erythrosine (>%95) was purcheased from Sigma Aldrich. The analytical grade ethanol, methanol, acetonitrile and 2-propanole were supplied from Merck (Darmstadt, Germany). Deionized water (0.05 μS·cm−1) was obtained from Millipore Elix 5 water purification system. Pasteurized cow milk obtained from markets in Turkey was used in production of C-nanodots. FeCl3 (>%99), sodium acetate (>%99) and analytical grade ethylene glycol were supplied from Carlo Erba. The X-ray diffraction (XRD) spectrums of C-nanodots and Fe3O4@C-nanodots hybrid materials were ensured using an advanced XRD diffractometer (Bruker AXS D8). The Raman analysis of C-nanodots and Fe3O4@C-nanodots hybrid materials were conducted by using Raman spectroscopy (WITec alpha 300 M + micro-Raman system with 532 nm laser source). The Shimadzu UV 1800 spectrophotometer was used for analysis of Erythrosine. A pH meter (Inolab, WTW pH720, Germany) and a vortex (Velp Scientifica ZX3, England) was used for extraction studies. For isolation and separation of magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots material, neodymium magnet was used.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots hybrid material
ce:section.ce:para: The production of C-nanodots was carried out according to our previous experiments [27], 20 mL of deionized pure water was added to 25 mL of the cow milk. The mixture was transferred to hydrothermal synthesis unit. The hydrothermal reactor as an easy and cheap nanomaterial production method was allowed to react at 180 °C for 12 h. The C-nanodots occurred at the end of the carbonization were separated from the liquid phase by centrifugation, washed with water and ethanol and dried in an oven at 75 °C. In the synthesis of magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots hybrid material, 1 g of FeCl3 and 4 g of sodium acetate was dissolved in 50 mL of ethylene glycol and 0.25 g of the previously produced C-nanodots were added to solution. The mixture was sonicated by ultrasonic bath for 15 min to distribute C-nanodots homogeneously into solution. The reaction solution was taken into the hydrothermal synthesis reactor and allowed to react for 8 h. in an oven set at 180 ℃. At the end of reaction, a neodymium magnet was used to separate the magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots from the liquid phase. The water phase separated from the solid phase then, distilled water was added to the solid phase twice and washing procedure was performed. The obtained magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots hybrid material was dried in the oven at 80 0C.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Magnetic solid phase extraction (MSPE) procedure for erythrosine
ce:section.ce:para: 20 mL of sample solution containing known concentration of erythrosine, pH 4.0 acetate buffer solution was placed in centrifuge tube. Then 7.5 mg of synthesized magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots were added into the sample solution. The mixture was kept on vortex at 3000 rpm for 10 min. After the adsorption process was completed, the particles was separated by a neodymium magnet and collected. Due to neodymium magnet has successful in taking out the magnetic particles from the liquid phase, the time was saved in the experimental stage by eliminating the need for centrifugation. The aqueous phase was removed and 1.0 mL of ethanol was added and kept on vortex for 2 min to elute adsorbed erythrosine on the surface of Fe3O4@C-nanodots. Elution process was applied three times and eluent phases were combined. The eluent phase was then analyzed by UV–VIS spectrophotometer at 536 nm to determine concentration of erythrosine. The same procedure was applied for the blank samples and standard solutions of calibration curve.

Adsorption experiments were carried out by mixing 20 mL of 20.0–5.0 mg·L−1 of erythrosine with 10.0 mg mass of adsorbent in clean conical flasks at optimum conditions. The mixtures were shaken at 3000 rpm on a vortex mixer for an adequate contact time at room temperature (25 °C). The amount of erythrosine adsorbed per gram of adsorbent, Qe (mg·g−1) was calculated using the following
(B.1)
Qe = ( Co - - Ce ) . V / W

where Qe is the adsorbent phase concentration after equilibrium (mg of erythrosine/g adsorbent), Co and Ce are the initial and final concentrations of erythrosine in solution (mg·L−1), V is the solution volume (L) and W is the sorbent mass (g). The results of adsoption experiments were obtained isotherm parameters for adsorption of erythrosine onto adsorbent.

The proposed method was applied to tablets and syrups as pharmaceutical forms taken from pharmacy at Turkey. The suggested method was directly applied to known volume of syrup sample. For the pharmaceutical tablet sample, 10 mL of methanol was added on the pharmaceutical tablet and the mixture obtained was mixed on the vortex mixer to extract erythrosine from tablet to methanol solution. The methanol phase was filtered and diluted to 20 mL with ultra-pure water. Then, the optimized MSPE method was applied these samples.

FE-SEM images of C-nanodots and magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots hybrid material with 3000x magnification ratio are shown in Fig. 1 . It is seen that the average particle size of the C-nanodots nanoparticles obtained by the hydrothermal method is in the range of 100–150 nm. As a result of the modification of C-nanodots with Fe3O4, it is seen that homogeneous magnetic particles have spherical structure with an average particle size of 100 nm are obtained [28].

XRD spectra of C-nanodots and Fe3O4@C-nanodots hybrid material are shown in Fig. 2 . The XRD spectra of the C-nanodots showed that the peaks observed at 2Θ: 15.3, 24.7, 35.9 and 48.0 diffraction angles belong to the C-nanodots. After the modification of the C-nanodots hybrid material with Fe3O4, it was proved that Fe3O4 magnetic particles were formed with diffraction angles of 2 Θ: 31.0, 35.7, 43.5 and 63.0, and when the XRD spectrum of the obtained hybrid material was examined, it was observed that the C-nanodots peaks were suppressed [29,30].

Raman spectra of C-nanodots and magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots are shown in Fig. S1. It has was proved by literature studies that the peaks at 1576 and 1346 cm−1 in the G and D bands belong to the C-nanodots. As a result of the modification of C-nanodots with Fe3O4 nanoparticles, it is clearly seen from the peaks at 218.5, 284.5, 397, 488 and 601 that the Fe3O4 magnetic particles are formed and also it has been proved that the G band is suppressed and the D band becomes more dominant, and a significant change in the ratio of D band peak intensity to G band peak intensity was achieved [30,31].

The adsorption of analyte/analytes in sample phase is maintance fenomenia at solid phase extraction applications. The pH of sample solutions is one of the most effective and important parameters. pH supplies the most suitable medium for adsorption of analyte to the adsorbent. Therefore, efficiency of pH of sample solution on the adsorption efficiency of erythrosine was checked in range of 3.0 and 7.0 by applying the MSPE method to model solutions. The results are given in Fig. 3 . According to our analysis results, maximum recovery and minimum standard deviation was obtained at pH 4.0. So optimum pH value for the aqueous sample phase was selected as pH 4.0 for the further experiments.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Effect of the amount of adsorbent
ce:section.ce:para: The amount of adsorbent must be optimum value to reach best adsorption and desorption efficiency for analyte. In this study, the effect of the amount of the adsorbent was checked by employing Fe3O4@C-nanodots doses changed from 3.0 mg to 20.0 mg. The results given in Fig. 4 showed that the highest recovery and the lowest standard deviation for erythrosine was obtained with addition of 7.5 mg of Fe3O4@C-nanodots. Therefore, further experiments were continued by using 7.5 mg of the adsorbent.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Effect of elution solvent type and volume
ce:section.ce:para: Desorption of erytrosine from the surface of Fe3O4@C-nanodot hybrid material was checked by using various solvents as shown in Fig. S2. According to results, ethanol is most suitable eluent for the desorption process. To found the optimum eluent volume, the developed method was applied at different volumes of ethanol ranging from 1.0 mL to 3.0 mL and sequential elution with 1.0 mL at three times. It can be seen from Fig. S3. that sequential desorption was most effective to desorption of erythrosine adsorbed on the magnetic Fe3O4@C-nanodots.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Effect of vortex time on adsorption process
ce:section.ce:para: The optimum interaction time between analyte and adsorbent is necessary for adsorption process because contact of analyte and adsorbent in sufficient time and effectively increases extraction efficiency. Fig. S4. showed the effect of vortex mixing time on the extraction efficiency of erythrosine. 5 min of vortex mixing of sample solution with Fe3O4@C-nanodots was enough to adsorption of erythrosine for quantitative recovery of erythrosine.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Effect of vortex time on desorption process
ce:section.ce:para: The suitable eluent solvent must be contact with the adsorbent for the effective elution of analyte from solid phase. The obtained results given in Fig. S5. showed that 2 min of vortex mixing time was enough for quantitative elution of erythrosine. The rapid completion of the elution process is an important advantage due to the high surface area of our nano-sized adsorbent.

Effect of sample volume on the experimental extraction performance of the proposed method was checked within the range of 10– 40 mL. The results given in Fig. S6. showed that 20 mLof sample volume provided the maximum recovery for erythrosine. Hence 20 mL of sample solution was used as optimum volume for next experimental studies.

Adsorption is defined through isotherms that connection the amount of analyte on the adsorbent. Distribution of analyte between the liquid phase and the solid phase can be described by different isotherm models such as Langmuir and Freundlich [32].

The Langmuir isotherm maintaine monolayer adsorption onto a surface containing sorption sites of uniform strategies with no transmigration of adsorbate in the surface. This indicates that the surface reaches a saturation point where the maximum adsorption of the surface will be achieved. The isotherm is represented by the linear plot of specific adsorption (Ce/qe) against the equilibrium concentration (Ce) shows that the adsorption obeys the Langmuir model. The constants b and qmax relate to the energy of adsorption and maximum adsorption capacity, and their values are obtained from the slope and interception of the plot and are presented in Table 1 . The Freundlich isotherm is introduced as an empirical model, qe represents the amount adsorbed per amount of adsorbent at the equilibrium (mg g−1), Ce represents the equilibrium concentration (mg L-1), and Kf and n are parameters that depend on the adsorbate and adsorbent. The Kf and n are Freundlich constants which correspond to adsorption capacity and adsorption intensity, respectively. Freundlich equilibrium constants were determined from the plot of ln qe versus ln Ce on the basis of the linear form of Freundlich equation. The n value indicates the degree of nonlinearity between solution concentration and adsorption as follows: if n = 1, then adsorption is linear; if n < 1 adsorption is a chemical process; if n > 1, then adsorption is a physical process [33,34].

The n value in Freundlich equation was found to be 9.65 (Table 1.). The situation n > 1 is most common and may be due to a distribution of surface sites or any factor that causes a decrease in adsorbentadsorbate interaction with increasing surface density and the values of n within the range of 1–10 represent good adsorption. In the present study, since n represent between 1 and 10 it indicates the physical adsorption of erythrosine onto magnetic C-nanodots [35].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analytical performance of the proposed MSPE method
ce:section.ce:para: The analytical performance parameters including limit of detection (LOD), limit of quantification (LOQ), linear dynamic range, intra-day and inter-day relative standard deviation values (RSD, %) are shown in Table 2 . The preconcentration factor (PF) was calculated by using the ratio of the highest model solution volume (20 mL) to the final volume. The LOD and LOQ were calculated according by ICH harmonized tripartite guideline that validation of analytical procedures [36]. Calculation of LOD and LOQ was based on the standard deviation of blank sample solution analysis.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Application of MSPE method to pharmaceutical samples
ce:section.ce:para: Validation of the developed MSPE procedure was checked by addition-recovery experiments for the spiked real samples under the optimum experimental conditions (Table S1). Therefore, known concentrations of erythrosine were added pharmaceutical tablet and syrup samples. The obtained recovery results are represented in Table 3 . The recoveries of the developed method were changing from 94.8% to 97.6%. The obtained results for real samples proved that our method was suitable and applicable for accurate analysis erythrosine in pharmaceutical tablet and syrup samples.

In this study, Fe3O4@C-nanodot hybrid material was synthesized by using green starting material and green synthesis approaches. The synthesized adsorbent was firstly used extraction of erythrosine. The developed MSPE technique was demonstrated to be an efficient strategy for the rapid extraction of erythrosine. A comparison of the proposed MSPE-UV–Vis procedure with some studies in literature is presented in Table 4 . As can be seen, our method has important analytical merits compare with the other ones. Some parameters are comparable or better than existed methods. So, it can be an alternative and practice application for fast and sensitive analysis of erythrosine in different matrix.

Elifcan Emiroğlu: Investigation, Data curation, Conceptualization, Writing – original draft, Writing - review & editing. Dönay Yuvali: Investigation, Data curation, Writing – original draft, Writing - review & editing. Gökhan Sarp: . Erkan Yilmaz: Investigation, Data curation, Conceptualization, Writing – original draft, Writing - review & editing. İbrahim Narin: Investigation, Data curation, Conceptualization, Writing – original draft, Writing - review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This research did not receive any specific grant from funding agencies in the public, commercial, or not-for-profit sectors.

ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.microc.2021.106766.
